article_title: China, Brazil, Italy and Belgium Join Nuclear Tripling Pledge at Paris Summit as 38 Nations Now Back 2050 Target

content_preview.title: China, Brazil, Italy and Belgium Join Nuclear Tripling Pledge at Paris Summit as 38 Nations Now Back 2050 Target
content_preview.summary: The second Nuclear Energy Summit in Paris brought four new signatories to the Declaration to Triple Nuclear Energy, with the EU announcing a 200 million euro guarantee for advanced reactor investment.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The second Nuclear Energy Summit, hosted by France and the International Atomic Energy Agency in Paris on March 10, concluded with four additional countries — China, Brazil, Italy and Belgium — [endorsing the Declaration to Triple Nuclear Energy](https://world-nuclear-news.org/articles/china-and-brazil-sign-up-to-tripling-nuclear-goal) by 2050. The coalition now stands at 38 nations, up from the [original 25 that signed](https://world-nuclear-news.org/articles/china-and-brazil-sign-...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News article at 667 words (within 400-1200 range). Clear section organization with thorough coverage of the summit outcomes.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 4 sources verified. IAEA: Confirmed von der Leyen 'strategic mistake' quote, US quadrupling pledge, 200M euro guarantee, 413 reactors/377 GW/69 under construction, Linglong One 2026, 'nearly 40 countries'. Elysée declaration: Confirmed 34 signatory countries, nuclear applications scope, deep geological disposal, regulatory framework. World Nuclear News: Confirmed Brazil + China + Italy + Belgium as four new signatories, 38 total, 25 original at COP28. Climate Change News: Confirmed Brazil ~90% clean electricity, Silveira Amazon SMR proposal, China 15-year capacity lead, Schneider exact quote on wind/solar.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Round 2 re-verification: The bot correctly disputed the Round 1 Brazil finding. Brazil's endorsement is confirmed by World Nuclear News and Climate Change News — it endorsed the tripling declaration even though it does not appear in the Elysée declaration's 34-country signatory list (the broader coalition is 38). The bot was also correct that the von der Leyen 'strategic mistake' quote and US quadrupling pledge ARE in the IAEA source — my Round 1 fetch missed them. All claims now have proper inline citations. The 140 companies/Google/Amazon/Meta claim was appropriately removed.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Balanced coverage including Germany's dissenting position with a direct, verified quote.
editor_notes.originality: No duplicate articles on this topic.
concerns: Two new sources (world-nuclear-news.org, climatechangenews.com) are not on the source allowlist — both are reputable specialist outlets and should be added
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Round 2: The rewrite comprehensively addresses all Round 1 findings. The bot correctly disputed the Brazil finding with evidence — Brazil IS confirmed as a new signatory by multiple independent sources. The bot also correctly identified that my Round 1 review missed the von der Leyen quote and US quadrupling pledge in the IAEA source. All claims now have proper inline citations to verified sources. The unsourced 140 companies claim was appropriately removed, and Germany's position is now properly sourced with a direct quote. Approved for publication.
